disclaimer: AI-assisted research from public web sources — indicative, verify before quoting a client.

ICON.note: Icon is a 646-unit, 99-year leasehold mixed-use development (2007) at 10 Gopeng Street sitting directly above Tanjong Pagar MRT (EW15). The wider Tanjong Pagar area is a long-term beneficiary of the Greater Southern Waterfront redevelopment as the port relocates to Tuas by 2027, though major nearby parcels only come online in the early 2030s.

SELETAR PARK RESIDENCE.note: Seletar Park Residence is a 276-unit, 99-year leasehold (2011) condo in Seletar Hills Estate developed by Asplenium Land, a Hong Leong Group entity, and completed in 2015. No en-bloc activity is reported and resale prices have been broadly flat around S$1,290 psf over 2023–2026.

ONE-NORTH RESIDENCES.note: One-North Residences is a 405-unit, 99-year leasehold mixed development (TOP 2009) beside one-north MRT (CC23), developed by a UOL/Kheng Leong/Low Keng Huat venture amid the Biopolis/Fusionopolis clusters. The precinct faces significant new supply, with Bloomsbury Residences (358 units) launched April 2025 and developers securing a further Dover/Slim Barracks-area site in 2026.

THE COAST AT SENTOSA COVE.note: The Coast at Sentosa Cove is a 249-unit, 99-year leasehold waterfront condo (completed 2009) transacting around S$1,530 psf with a high share of loss-making resales. Sentosa Cove non-landed prices fell roughly 18.7% from the 2023 peak, weighed by the 60% foreigner ABSD raised in April 2023, with recent data showing early signs of recovery.

LUMIERE.note: Lumiere is a 168-unit, 45-storey, 99-year leasehold development (2010) at 2 Mistri Road, Tanjong Pagar, within walking distance of Tanjong Pagar, Telok Ayer and Raffles Place MRT stations. Like nearby projects it is a longer-term beneficiary of the Greater Southern Waterfront masterplan following the port's relocation to Tuas by 2027.

THE CLIFT.note: Completed in 2011 by Far East Organization, Singapore's largest private developer, The Clift is a 312-unit, 99-year leasehold tower at 21 McCallum Street. It sits a short walk from Tanjong Pagar (EWL) and Telok Ayer (DTL) MRT stations in the Raffles Place/Tanjong Pagar CBD.

SPOTTISWOODE RESIDENCES.note: This 351-unit freehold development at 48 Spottiswoode Park Road will benefit from the new Cantonment MRT (Circle Line Stage 6) opening 12 July 2026, which closes the Circle Line loop. Recent transactions reached up to about S$2,582 psf in May 2026, while the adjacent Spottiswoode Park HDB estate is not slated for SERS after HDB's August 2025 statement that no further SERS sites are planned.

WALLICH RESIDENCE.note: Singapore's tallest residential development (181 units on floors 39–64 of Guoco Tower), completed 2018 by GuocoLand with direct access to Tanjong Pagar MRT. Its triplex super penthouse was bought by James Dyson for S$73.8m then sold at a loss for S$62m to Leo Koguan in 2020.

JOOL SUITES.note: Jool Suites is a 52-unit freehold development at 2 Sing Joo Walk in the Kampong Java/Farrer Park area, completed around 2013–2014 by SJ Capital and within roughly a 4-minute walk of Farrer Park MRT (NEL). The newer 8 Farrer Suites sits next door at 8 Sing Joo Walk, with City Square Mall nearby.

10 SHELFORD.note: 10 Shelford is a 69-unit freehold development at 10 Shelford Road, Bukit Timah (District 11), completed in 2014 by Adam Properties. The nearest MRT stations are Tan Kah Kee and Botanic Gardens on the Downtown and Circle Lines.
